id_policy: IDs frozen on first assignment; reorder via 'order', insert by appending a new seq.

gpeter.name: Gospel of Peter (Akhmim)
gpeter.note: Sections 1-60; passion/burial/guard only — resurrection-witness embellishment excluded.

ghebrews.name: Gospel of the Hebrews
ghebrews.note: Patristic citations; addressed by citing father.

pericopes.title: The boy Jesus in the temple

witnesses.ref: Gospel of the Hebrews — baptism fragment (Jerome on Isa 11:2)

notes.placement: DISPUTED PLACEMENT. John places the cleansing at the start; Synoptics at the final week (Part X).

pericopes.title: The Samaritan woman at the well
Return to Galilee; the official's son

notes.placement: DISPUTED PLACEMENT. Luke places Nazareth rejection at ministry opening; Mark 6:1-6 later (Part VII).

pericopes.title: The paralytic let down through the roof

endnotes.body: Anthropological faculty vs. received divine Spirit; do not disambiguate via capitalization.

pericopes.title: Call of Levi (Matthew); eating with sinners

witnesses.note: The physician scene with 'and priests' addition. Other clauses of this fragment: VIII.8 (not against you), IV.3 (pray for enemies).
Primary home for Thomas 47 (wineskins/patch + two masters). Part IV two-masters is cross-ref only.

pericopes.title: The man with the withered hand

parts.label: IV
parts.title: The Sermon (on the Mount / on the Plain)

pericopes.title: Law and the prophets; the antitheses

witnesses.note: The 'pray for enemies' clause of P.Oxy 1224 Frag 2 recto col. ii; primary text at III.6.
'Not I, but the Lord' — Paul citing the dominical command on divorce.
Flag the 'mysteries' frame.

pericopes.title: Treasure in heaven; serving two masters; anxiety

witnesses.note: Thomas 47 inserted whole at III.7 (fasting); pointer only here.

pericopes.title: Judging; the speck and the log

witnesses.note: The dominical cluster: 'Be ye merciful... forgive... with what measure ye mete.'
'Do not judge... forgive... with what measure you measure.'

pericopes.title: Ask, seek, knock; the golden rule
The narrow gate; tree and fruit; two foundations
Raising the widow's son at Nain
John the Baptist's question; Jesus's reply
Thanksgiving to the Father; "come to me"
Women who followed and supported Jesus
Lamp under a basket; the measure
Jairus's daughter and the hemorrhaging woman

witnesses.note: Parallels Matt 9:37-38; cross-ref IX.6 (the seventy).
'The Lord commanded that preachers be supported' — Paul citing the dominical rule.

pericopes.title: Tradition of the elders; clean and unclean

witnesses.note: Egerton Frag 2 recto presented whole at X.9 (Tribute to Caesar); the Isaiah 'lips/heart' clause is part of that fragment and is not split out.

pericopes.title: Demand for a sign; leaven of the Pharisees

parts.label: VIII
parts.title: The Turning Point — Confession to Transfiguration

witnesses.note: Flag the secret 'three things' and 'bubbling spring' frame.

pericopes.title: First passion prediction; taking up the cross
The boy with an unclean spirit

witnesses.note: Anchored via the mountain-moving saying (Matt 17:20). Flag the 'make the two one / sons of man' unification frame.

pericopes.title: Who is the greatest; the child

witnesses.ref: P.Oxy 1224, frag. 2 recto col. ii — 'not against you' clause
witnesses.note: Second clause of P.Oxy 1224 Frag 2 recto col. ii. The physician clause is at III.6; the 'pray for enemies' clause cross-refs IV.3.

pericopes.title: Woes against the Pharisees and lawyers
Fear God; confess Christ; the unforgivable word

witnesses.note: P.Oxy 5575 opening clause ('the rich man died'). Full fragment at IV.7; pointer only.

pericopes.title: Healing the bent woman on the Sabbath
The narrow door; lament over Jerusalem
At a Pharisee's table; places of honor; the great banquet

witnesses.note: Primary home for Thomas 64. Part X wedding banquet is cross-ref only.
P.Oxy 5575 'fast from the world' clause. Full fragment at IV.7; pointer only.

pericopes.title: The dishonest manager; the law; the rich man and Lazarus
Faith, duty, and the ten lepers

witnesses.note: Flag the Salome dialogue and 'from the undivided' tail.

pericopes.title: The persistent widow; the Pharisee and tax collector
"I am the light of the world"; the controversies

witnesses.note: Anchored to 'I am the light of the world' (John 8:12). Flag the 'I am the All' immanence.

witnesses.ref: Egerton Gospel, Fragment 1 verso (controversy / 'search the scriptures')
witnesses.note: The lawyers/rulers controversy including 'Search the scriptures' and the stoning attempt.

pericopes.title: The Good Shepherd; the Feast of Dedication
The raising of Lazarus; the plot

witnesses.note: Dominical divorce command; primary canonical witness at IV.4.
Flag the 'make the two one / male not male' unification tail.

pericopes.title: The rich young ruler; reward of discipleship

witnesses.note: Anchored via 'the first will be last' (Mark 10:31). Flag the 'old man / child of seven days' and 'become one' frame.

pericopes.title: The request of James and John; servant leadership
The parable of the pounds / talents

notes.placement: DISPUTED PLACEMENT. Synoptics place cleansing in final week; John (II.3) at ministry start.

witnesses.note: Logion 66 follows 65 in Thomas exactly as the rejected-stone citation follows the parable in Mark 12:10-11.
Thomas 64 inserted at IX.13 (great banquet); pointer only here.

witnesses.ref: Egerton Gospel, Fragment 2 recto (tribute question + Isaiah clause)
witnesses.note: Presented whole here (both the tribute question and the Isaiah 'lips/heart' clause). The Isaiah clause parallels VII.12 (Mark 7:6-7) but travels with the fragment.

pericopes.title: Woes on the scribes and Pharisees

witnesses.note: P.Oxy 840 Temple-purity controversy; primary home is B.2. Cross-referenced here for the inside/outside-purity theme.

pericopes.title: Greeks seek Jesus; the voice from heaven
The Olivet discourse (the end of the age)

witnesses.note: 'A word of the Lord' on the coming; canonical prophetic tradition.
Anchored via door/bridal-chamber imagery. Flag the 'solitary' (monachos) motif.
The earliest written account of the institution; Paul cites received tradition.

pericopes.title: Before Annas and Caiaphas; the Sanhedrin

witnesses.note: Parallels the temple-destruction charge (Mark 14:58 // 15:29). Fragmentary logion.
Pilate and Herod; Joseph requests the body; 'The sun shouldn't set on one who's been killed.'
The mockery; 'Let's drag the Son of God'; thorn crown; crucifixion between wrongdoers; the penitent wrongdoer.
The darkness over Judea; 'My Power, the Power, you've left me!'; veil of the temple torn.
Nails drawn; earthquake; 'Joseph's Garden'; 'Woe to our sins.'
Guard at the tomb; Petronius; seven seals. Matches Matt 27:62-66.

notes.placement: Gospel of Peter §§34-49 (resurrection spectacle: giant figures, talking cross) was considered and excluded — see 'What Has Been Left Out'.

witnesses.note: Mary Magdalene and friends; the young man announces the resurrection.

pericopes.title: By the Sea of Tiberias; Peter restored

witnesses.note: The disciples return to the sea; Simon Peter, Andrew, and Levi son of Alphaeus — the fragment breaks off.

witnesses.ref: Gospel of the Hebrews — James fragment (Jerome, De vir. ill. 2)
witnesses.note: Jerome, De viris inlustribus 2: the appearance to James. 'My brother, eat thy bread, for the Son of man is risen.'

pericopes.title: Peter in the house of Cornelius
"More blessed to give than to receive"

witnesses.note: Temple-purity controversy. Cross-referenced at X.13.

witnesses.ref: P.Oxy 1224, frag. 2 recto col. i — unanchored opening clause
witnesses.note: The 'It weighed me down... Why are you discouraged?' opening has no synoptic home. The two legible clauses are now placed: physician at III.6, 'not against you' at VIII.8.
Full fragment inserted at IV.7 (anxiety/two-masters). Retained here as the transcription home with cross-references at IX.7 (rich fool) and IX.14 (fast from world).

witnesses.ref: Egerton Gospel, Fragment 2 verso (Jordan nature miracle)
witnesses.note: The fragmentary nature miracle at the Jordan — Jesus sprinkles water and 'sent forth fruit'; no synoptic parallel.
Eucharistic prayers; borderline status. Cross-referenced at XI.4 (Institution).

pericopes.title: Gospel of the Hebrews — unanchored sayings.

witnesses.ref: Gospel of the Hebrews — Tabor/hair saying (Origen, Comm. John 2.12.87)
witnesses.note: Origen, Comm. John 2.12.87: 'my mother, the Holy Spirit, take me by one of my hairs and carry me away on to the great mountain Tabor.'

witnesses.ref: Gospel of the Hebrews — seek-find saying (Clement)
witnesses.note: Clement, Strom. 2.9.45.5 / 5.14.96.3: 'He that seeks will not rest until he finds; and he that has found shall marvel...' Parallels Thomas 2.

witnesses.ref: Gospel of the Hebrews — brother-with-love saying (Jerome, Comm. Eph. 3)
witnesses.note: Jerome, Comm. Eph. 3: 'never be ye joyful, save when ye behold your brother with love.'

witnesses.ref: Gospel of the Hebrews — grieved-spirit saying (Jerome, Comm. Ezek. 6)
witnesses.note: Jerome, Comm. Ezek. 6: 'He that has grieved the spirit of his brother.'

pericopes.title: Thomas sayings without a narrative anchor.

left_out.title: What Has Been Left Out
left_out.scope: New Testament canon material not admitted to the Basileian Canon, with rationale. Omission reflects this canon's reporting criterion (ministry-to-ascension, reported as fact), not a verdict on the wider NT.
left_out.note: Non-canonical material considered and excluded (e.g. Gospel of Peter §§34-49, the 17 excluded Thomas logia, Didache 1:2-6 and 8:2-3, the Gospel of the Hebrews incarnation fragment) is documented at its place in the working document and in the Thomas triage.

categories.label: Pre-ministry material
categories.reason: Precedes the canon's starting point (John the Baptist's preaching, Part I). The only pre-ministry pericope retained is the boy Jesus in the temple (Luke 2:41-52, Pr.1).

items.note: Genealogy, nativity, Magi, flight to Egypt, Herod.
Annunciations, birth of John, nativity, shepherds, presentation, Simeon and Anna, genealogy.
The Logos hymn; theological prologue, not narrated ministry. Johannine material begins at John 1:19.

categories.label: Post-ascension material
categories.reason: Falls beyond the terminus (the ascension: Mark 16:19; Luke 24:50-53; Acts 1:9-11).

items.note: Reports the apostles' activity, not Jesus's earthly ministry. Retained: Acts 1:1-11 (forty days + ascension boundary, XIII.10); kerygmatic summaries 2:22-24, 10:36-43, 13:23-31 (Appendix A); agraphon 20:35 (B.1).
Apocalyptic vision of the exalted Christ; post-ascension and visionary. The words to the seven churches (Rev 2-3) are given in vision after the ascension.

categories.label: Interpretation and theology rather than reporting (the Epistles)
categories.reason: The letters expound the significance of Jesus; they do not narrate his deeds or teaching. Excluded as a class, except explicit dominical commands or early reporting-formulas, which are admitted at their place in the harmony.

items.ref: Romans, 2 Corinthians, Galatians, Ephesians, Philippians, Colossians, 2 Thessalonians, 1-2 Timothy, Titus, Philemon, Hebrews, James, 1-2 Peter, 1-3 John, Jude
items.note: Excluded in full. Note: the Christ-hymns Phil 2:6-11 and Col 1:15-20 interpret rather than report, and James echoes but does not narrate dominical speech.
Retained: 7:10-11 (divorce, 'not I, but the Lord'); 9:14 (support of preachers); 11:23-26 (the Supper); 15:3-7 (resurrection-appearance formula).
Retained: 4:15-17 ('a word of the Lord' on the coming).

categories.label: Text-critical exclusions
categories.reason: Absent from the working Greek text (Westcott-Hort 1881).

items.note: Absent from the earliest manuscripts; relegated in WH. The harmony passes from John 7:52 to 8:12.
Text-critically contested but retained as parallel material with a text-critical note, not omitted.
Contested but retained with a text-critical note.
